title: qPCR for Monitoring Fish Species Authenticity in Processed Seafood
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Seafood     mislabeling     and     species     substitution   pose   significant     challenges     to     food     authenticity  ,   consumer     trust  ,   and     public     health  .   qPCR   (  quantitative     Polymerase     Chain     Reaction  )   offers     a     sensitive     and     specific     approach     for     identifying     fish     species   in   raw  ,   processed  ,   and     mixed     seafood     products  . By   targeting     species-specific   DNA   sequences  ,   qPCR     can     rapidly     authenticate     products     within     hours  ,   preventing     fraudulent     substitution     and     ensuring     compliance     with     labeling     regulations  .   Its     application     also     supports     the     protection     of     endangered     species  ,   traceability   in   the     supply     chain  ,   and     fair     market     competition  .   Integrating     qPCR     into     national     seafood     monitoring     programs     will     enhance     consumer     confidence  ,   protect     economic     interests  ,   and     strengthen     regulatory     oversight  .   This     policy     brief     highlights     the     urgent     need     for     standardized     qPCR     protocols     and     strategic     investment     to     enable     reliable     fish     species     authentication     at     scale  .
